creator: tvrusso@sandia.gov
text:
Per the comments in the PR:

  From-To:        gerald->bde
  By:     gerald
  When:   Fri Nov 17 09:36:24 UTC 2006
  Why:    This is not a problem with GCC/GNU Fortran, but libm as far as I understood. 

Perhaps there's a problem with libm to go with this issue ("libm
doesn't have fmodl and it should" or something), it seems to me that there's a
problem with GCC/GNU Fortran if it tries to generate references to the
non-existant function in a library it has a chance to probe at the
time the compiler is built, even when compiling a program that does no
math.  At any rate, the fortran compiler produced by this particular
PORTVERSION is unusable, as it will not even compile a "Hello World"
on FreeBSD 5.5 (perhaps it works on 6.x?).  

This was NOT the case in the previous version of the port I had before
portupgrading, which was 20061024.  Versions between that version and 
20061107 failed to build in i386 because of a mistake in the BROKEN value, so
gcc-4.2.0-20061024 is the last version I know to have worked for sure.

I'm also sad to report that today, having given up on gfortran-4.2.0,
I deinstalled the port and installed gcc41 with fortran, which had
worked for me several months ago.  Now it, too, is giving the same
undefined reference to fmodl.  That gcc package is 4.1.2-20061110.
This leaves me with no gcc-4.x compiler that I can use with fortran.
